meta.title: Server-Side Rendering (SSR) Deep Dive
meta.excerpt: Dynamic, request-time rendering for personalized and real-time content. Learn when and how to optimize SSR for maximum performance.

props.content: SSR generates HTML on the server for each request, allowing you to render dynamic, personalized content while still providing fully formed HTML to search engines and users.

Unlike SSG where pages are built once, SSR renders pages fresh on every request. This enables real-time data, user authentication, personalization, and dynamic content that changes based on the request context.

steps.title: Server Process
steps.description: Server fetches data and renders HTML

leftItems: Real-time data displays (stock prices, live scores)
rightItems: High traffic with identical content (use ISR)
Static shell with dynamic parts (use PPR)

props.content: In Next.js App Router, SSR is triggered when you use dynamic functions like cookies(), headers(), searchParams, or set dynamic = 'force-dynamic'.
Access request-time data to trigger SSR:
SSR adds server processing time to each request. Here are strategies to optimize performance:

features.title: Parallel Data Fetching
features.description: Use Promise.all() to fetch multiple data sources simultaneously instead of sequentially.

features.title: Streaming with Suspense
features.description: Wrap slow components in Suspense to stream HTML progressively and show loading states.

features.title: Cache Where Appropriate
features.description: Use fetch caching or React cache() for data that doesn't need to be real-time.

features.title: Edge Runtime
features.description: Deploy SSR functions to edge locations to reduce latency for global users.

props.content: In our electrical services application, we use SSR for pages that require user-specific or real-time data:

features.title: Quote form
features.description: Captures user session data for prefilling

features.title: Search results
features.description: Dynamic filtering based on query params

props.content: Use SSR when content must be personalized, authenticated, or real-time. Optimize with parallel fetching, streaming with Suspense boundaries, and appropriate caching strategies to minimize server response time. Remember that every millisecond of server processing time adds to the user's first contentful paint.
